title: Wormhole: from memory to markets
subtitle: Explore an idea until a wormhole drops you somewhere unexpected.

steps.title: Memory, as a network
steps.body: 1982. Hopfield shows how a network of simple units can store and recall patterns — associative memory: a system settling into a remembered state. We're not headed anywhere in particular. Let's just follow this idea and see where it goes.

steps.title: Learning the associations
steps.body: The next question is how a network LEARNS its associations, not just stores them. We follow a citation to the Boltzmann machine (Hinton & Sejnowski, 1985) — a network that learns the very patterns it settles into. Its details open on the left.

steps.title: Recognizing patterns
steps.body: Keep going. By 1989 the thread reaches a synthesis — "Neural network models for pattern recognition and associative memory." We've stayed inside one idea the whole way: store, learn, recognize. This is where it gets strange.

steps.title: A wormhole
steps.body: Not every link is a citation. A violet conduit blooms from this node — a semantic edge to a near-identical idea that never cited it, in a field you'd never think to check. Where does it lead?

steps.title: Corporate bankruptcy
steps.body: Business and finance. "Financial distress prediction using the hybrid associative memory" (2016). The same recall mechanism that models how a brain settles into a memory is here predicting which companies will collapse. A 1982 idea from neuroscience, alive in corporate finance — and the citation graph would never have shown you.

steps.title: Through the wormhole
steps.body: Cross the semantic edge into the other field — the jump no citation chain ever offered. Kinship the graph found by meaning, not by reference.

steps.title: Found by exploring
steps.body: We started on a 1982 model of memory, followed it through learning and pattern recognition, and a wormhole dropped us somewhere no one was looking: predicting financial collapse. That's the point — you don't search for the surprise, you explore until it finds you.
